discussion_posts.name: olegmak
discussion_posts.content: One of the rare interview problems that actually tests real-life programmer skills and not competitive programming skills.

discussion_posts.name: logingood
discussion_posts.content: this question should be medium I guess ? Probably even easier than LRU cache.

discussion_posts.name: ameypalav
discussion_posts.content: Asked to me in Doordash technical screening round on 5/1/2025

discussion_posts.name: lambda826
discussion_posts.content:
input:
["FileSystem","mkdir","ls","ls","mkdir","ls","ls","addContentToFile","ls","ls","ls"]
[[],["/goowmfn"],["/goowmfn"],["/"],["/z"],["/"],["/"],["/goowmfn/c","shetopcy"],["/z"],["/goowmfn/c"],["/goowmfn"]]

output:
[null,null,[],["goowmfn"],null,["goowmfn","z"],["goowmfn","z"],null,[],["shetopcy"],["c"]]

expected:
[null,null,[],["goowmfn"],null,["goowmfn","z"],["goowmfn","z"],null,[],["c"],["c"]]


Can any explain why the last 2th ls("/goowmfn/c") shouldn't output “shetopcy” ？


ps:
root - goowmfn
                -c ("shetopcy")
        - z

discussion_posts.name: MaliGoBrrBrr
discussion_posts.content: whoever is saying this is medium ... just how? I found this to be harder than LRU , even harder than LFU because the sheer amount of things we have to deal with.

discussion_posts.name: code__HARD
discussion_posts.content: Similar to a TRIE DataStructure.\nThe difficult part is understanding the ls function.

discussion_posts.name: msseekers
discussion_posts.content: I have seen Leetcode framing the question in wrong way multiple times and here it\'s more on LLD side for e.g. when this question was asked to me in Amazon it was bar raiser and he was expecting LLD + DS for the same and that too with how level code that can be extended to add more command and feature for e.g. find command with different parameter.

discussion_posts.name: phillipc
discussion_posts.content: The problem description is unclear and misleading:\n\t\u2022\tIt never defines how to distinguish a file path from a directory path, yet this distinction is central to ls().\n\t\u2022\tThe behavior of addContentToFile() is inconsistent with mkdir(). It silently expects you to create missing parent directories, but the prompt doesn\u2019t say so \u2014 unlike mkdir(), which does.

discussion_posts.name: alementuev
discussion_posts.content: Jimfs is an in-memory file system for Java 7 and above, implementing the java.nio.file abstract file system APIs.\nhttps://github.com/google/jimfs
